---
title: "Final"
output: html_document
date: "2026-07-01"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# =====================================================
# STEP 0 — Load Required Packages
# =====================================================

## Data processing
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(zoo)

## Spatial analysis
library(geosphere)

## Visualization
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(reshape2)
library(corrplot)

## Clustering
library(factoextra)

## Machine learning
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(e1071)
library(nnet)
library(Metrics)

## SHAP analysis
library(kernelshap)
library(shapviz)
```

# 4.1.1
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.1.1 Monitoring Station Selection
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Load Monitoring Station Coordinates

station_loc <- read_excel(
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Station Location.xlsx"
) %>%
  rename(
    Station = Station_ID, Lat = Latitude, Lon = Longitude
  )

# PJ meteorological station coordinates
pj_lat <- 3.1
pj_lon <- 101.65


# STEP 2 — Calculate Distance from Each Monitoring Station to the PJ Meteorological Station

station_loc <- station_loc %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    Distance_km = distHaversine(
      c(Lon, Lat),
      c(pj_lon, pj_lat)
    ) / 1000
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# STEP 3 — Select Monitoring Stations within a 20 km Radius

selected_stations <- station_loc %>%
  filter(Distance_km <= 20) %>%
  rename(
    `Distance to PJ (km)` = Distance_km
  )

head(selected_stations)

# STEP 4 — Export Selected Monitoring Stations

write.csv(
  selected_stations,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Selected_Stations_20km.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 1 — Interactive Map of Selected Monitoring Stations
# =====================================================

# STEP 5 — Load Interactive Mapping Package
library(leaflet)

# STEP 6 — Create Station Selection Indicator
station_map <- station_loc

station_map$Selection <- ifelse(
  station_map$Station %in% selected_stations$Station,
  "Selected",
  "Not Selected"
)

# STEP 7 — Convert Selection Variable to Factor
station_map$Selection <- as.factor(station_map$Selection)

# STEP 8 — Define Color Palette for Station Classification
colors <- c("#1E3A8A", "#DC2626")

pal <- colorFactor(
  palette = colors,
  domain = station_map$Selection
)

# STEP 9 — Generate Interactive Monitoring Station Map
map <- leaflet(station_map) %>%
  
  addTiles() %>%
  
  addCircleMarkers(
    
    lng = ~Lon,
    lat = ~Lat,
    
    color = ~pal(Selection),
    fillColor = ~pal(Selection),
    
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    radius = 7,
    weight = 1,
    
    popup = ~paste(
      "<b>Station ID:</b>", Station, "<br>",
      "<b>Selection:</b>", Selection, "<br>",
      "<b>Distance to PJ (km):</b>",
      round(Distance_km, 2)
    )
  ) %>%
  
  addLegend(
    pal = pal,
    values = ~Selection,
    title = "Station Selection",
    position = "bottomright"
  )

# STEP 10 — Display Interactive Map
map
```

# 4.1.2
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.1.2 Temperature Data Processing
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Import Daily Temperature Data

temp_raw <- read_excel(
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/PJ Daily Temp 2018-2024.10.xls"
)

# STEP 2 — Construct Date Variable and Clean Temperature Data

temp_daily <- temp_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(
      ISOdate(Year, Month, Day)
    ),
    # Treat negative temperature values as missing
    Temperature = ifelse(
      Temperature < 0,
      NA,
      Temperature
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(Date)

# STEP 3 — Impute Missing Temperature Values

temp_daily <- temp_daily %>%
  mutate(
    # Linear interpolation
    Temp_t = na.approx(Temperature, na.rm = FALSE
    )
  ) %>%
  # Forward fill remaining missing values
  mutate(
    Temp_t = na.locf(
      Temp_t,
      na.rm = FALSE
    )
  ) %>%
  # Backward fill remaining missing values
  mutate(
    Temp_t = na.locf(
      Temp_t, fromLast = TRUE
    )
  )

# STEP 4 — Calculate 60-Day Moving Average Temperature (TM60)

temp_daily <- temp_daily %>%
  mutate(
    TM60 = rollapplyr(
      Temp_t, width = 61, FUN = mean, fill = NA,
      partial = TRUE
    )
  )

# STEP 5 — Export Temperature Features

write.csv(
  temp_daily,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Temperature_Features.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 2 — Temperature Exploratory Analysis
# =====================================================

# STEP 6 — Daily Temperature Time Series

ggplot(temp_daily, aes(x = Date, y = Temp_t)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Temperature Series",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Temperature (°C)"
  )

# STEP 7 — Prepare Data for Temperature Distribution Comparison

temp_long <- temp_daily %>%
  select(
    TM60, Temp_t
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Temperature"
  )

# STEP 8 — Temperature Distribution Comparison

ggplot(temp_long, aes(x = Temperature, fill = Type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature Distribution Comparison",
    x = "Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Variable"
  )
```

# 4.1.3
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.1.3 Rainfall Data Processing
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Read Rainfall Data

rain_raw <- read_excel(
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/PJ Daily Rainfall 2015-2024.10.xls"
)

# STEP 2 — Construct Date Variable and Clean Rainfall Data

rain_daily <- rain_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(
      ISOdate(Year, Month, Day)
    ),
    # Convert trace rainfall (-33.3) to small value
    Rainfall = ifelse(
      Rainfall == -33.3, 0.05, Rainfall
    ),
    # Treat missing rainfall (-1.1) as NA
    Rainfall = ifelse(
      Rainfall == -1.1, NA, Rainfall
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(Date)

# STEP 3 — Impute Missing Rainfall Using Local Median

rain_daily <- rain_daily %>%
  mutate(
    Rain_t = ifelse(
      is.na(Rainfall),

      rollapply(
        Rainfall, width = 7, FUN = median, fill = NA,
        align = "center", na.rm = TRUE
      ),

      Rainfall
    )
  )

# STEP 4 — Generate Rainfall Lag Variables (0–14 Days)

rain_daily <- rain_daily %>%
  mutate(
    lag0  = lag(Rain_t, 0),
    lag1  = lag(Rain_t, 1),
    lag2  = lag(Rain_t, 2),
    lag3  = lag(Rain_t, 3),
    lag4  = lag(Rain_t, 4),
    lag5  = lag(Rain_t, 5),
    lag6  = lag(Rain_t, 6),
    lag7  = lag(Rain_t, 7),
    lag8  = lag(Rain_t, 8),
    lag9  = lag(Rain_t, 9),
    lag10 = lag(Rain_t, 10),
    lag11 = lag(Rain_t, 11),
    lag12 = lag(Rain_t, 12),
    lag13 = lag(Rain_t, 13),
    lag14 = lag(Rain_t, 14)
  )

# STEP 5 — Export Rainfall Features

write.csv(
  rain_daily,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Rainfall_Features.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 3 — Rainfall Exploratory Analysis
# =====================================================

# STEP 6 — Daily Rainfall Time Series

ggplot(rain_daily, aes(x = Date, y = Rain_t)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Rainfall Series",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Rainfall (mm)"
  )

# STEP 7 — Prepare Rainfall Lag Data for Distribution Analysis

rain_density_df <- rain_daily %>%
  select(
    lag0,
    lag1, lag2, lag3, lag4, lag5, lag6, lag7,
    lag8, lag9, lag10, lag11, lag12, lag13, lag14
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Rainfall"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = factor(
      Variable,
      levels = paste0("lag", 0:14)
    )
  )

# STEP 8 — Rainfall Lag Density Distributions

ggplot(
  rain_density_df,
  aes(
    x = Rainfall,
    fill = Variable,
    color = Variable
  )
) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Variable, ncol = 4, scales = "free"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Density Distributions of Rainfall Lag Variables",
    x = "Rainfall (mm)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )
```

# 4.1.4
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.1.4 Data Matching and Statistical Analysis
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Import Basin Water Quality Dataset

basin_df <- read_excel(
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Basin Klang_Database 2018-2024.xlsx"
) %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(Date),
    row_id = row_number()
  )

# STEP 2 — Define Water Quality Variables

wq_cols <- c(
  "DO", "PH", "BOD", "COD", "AN", "SS", "WQI"
)

# STEP 3 — Handle Left-Censored Values (<x)

# Convert "<x" values to 0.5 × x
basin_df <- basin_df %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(wq_cols),
      ~ ifelse(
        grepl("^<", as.character(.)),
        as.numeric(sub("<", "", as.character(.))) * 0.5,
        as.character(.)
      )
    )
  )

# STEP 4 — Convert Water Quality Variables to Numeric Format

basin_df <- basin_df %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(wq_cols),
      ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.))
    )
  )

# STEP 5 — Prepare Temperature Features

temp_features <- temp_daily %>%
  select(
    Date,
    Temp_t,
    TM60
  )

# STEP 6 — Prepare Rainfall Features

rain_features <- rain_daily %>%
  select(
    Date,
    lag0, lag1, lag2, lag3, lag4,
    lag5, lag6, lag7, lag8, lag9,
    lag10, lag11, lag12, lag13, lag14
  )

# STEP 7 — Merge Water Quality, Temperature, and Rainfall Datasets

final_matched_df <- basin_df %>%
  left_join(temp_features, by = "Date") %>%
  left_join(rain_features, by = "Date") %>%
  arrange(row_id) %>%
  select(-row_id)

# STEP 8 — Export Final Analytical Dataset

write.csv(
  final_matched_df,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Final_Matched_Dataset.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# STEP 9 — WQI Summary Statistics by Monitoring Station

station_wqi_summary <- final_matched_df %>%
  group_by(Station_ID1) %>%
  summarise(
    N = sum(!is.na(WQI)),
    Mean_WQI = mean(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_WQI = sd(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_WQI = min(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median_WQI = median(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_WQI = max(WQI, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_WQI))

station_wqi_summary

write.csv(
  station_wqi_summary,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/WQI_Summary_By_Station.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# STEP 10 — Climate Variable Summary Statistics

climate_summary <- final_matched_df %>%
  select(
    Temp_t,
    TM60,
    lag0, lag1, lag2, lag3, lag4,
    lag5, lag6, lag7, lag8, lag9,
    lag10, lag11, lag12, lag13, lag14
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = factor(
      Variable,
      levels = c(
        "Temp_t",
        "TM60",
        paste0("lag", 0:14)
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(Variable) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Variable)

climate_summary

write.csv(
  climate_summary,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Climate_Summary.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 4 — Hydro-meteorological Correlation Structure
# =====================================================

# STEP 14 — Prepare Hydro-meteorological Variables for Correlation Analysis

vars_fig2 <- final_matched_df %>%
  select(
    WQI,
    lag0, lag1, lag2, lag3, lag4,
    lag5, lag6, lag7, lag8, lag9,
    lag10, lag11, lag12, lag13, lag14,
    Temp_t,
    TM60
  )

# STEP 15 — Compute Correlation Matrix

corr_fig2 <- cor(
  vars_fig2,
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)

# STEP 16 — Reshape Correlation Matrix for Visualization

corr_melt <- reshape2::melt(corr_fig2)

# STEP 17 — Plot Hydro-meteorological Correlation Heatmap

ggplot(
  corr_melt,
  aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)
) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(value, 2)),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      size = 8
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hydro-meteorological Lag Structure and WQI",
    x = "",
    y = ""
  )
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 5 — WQI Spatial Distribution Across Stations
# =====================================================

# STEP 18 — WQI Distribution by Monitoring Station

ggplot(
  final_matched_df,
  aes(
    x = reorder(
      Station_ID1,
      WQI,
      median,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    y = WQI
  )
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "WQI Distribution Across Monitoring Stations",
    x = "Station",
    y = "WQI"
  )
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 6 — WQI Distribution Density
# =====================================================

# STEP 19 — WQI Density Distribution

ggplot(
  final_matched_df,
  aes(x = WQI)
) +
  geom_density(
    fill = "lightblue",
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = mean(final_matched_df$WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    colour = "red",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = median(final_matched_df$WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    colour = "darkgreen",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Density Distribution of WQI",
    x = "WQI",
    y = "Density"
  )
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 7 — Water Quality Parameter Correlation Structure
# =====================================================

# STEP 20 — Prepare Water Quality Variables

water_vars <- final_matched_df %>%
  select(
    DO, PH, BOD, COD, AN, SS, WQI
  )

# STEP 21 — Compute Correlation Matrix

water_cor <- cor(
  water_vars,
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)

# STEP 22 — Reshape Correlation Matrix

water_melt <- reshape2::melt(water_cor)

# STEP 23 — Plot Water Quality Correlation Heatmap

ggplot(
  water_melt,
  aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)
) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(value, 2)),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation among Water Quality Parameters",
    x = "",
    y = ""
  )
```

# 4.2
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.2 Rainfall Lag Variables Analysis
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Prepare Dataset for Correlation Analysis

lag_corr_data <- final_matched_df %>%
  select(
    WQI,
    lag0, lag1, lag2, lag3, lag4,
    lag5, lag6, lag7, lag8, lag9,
    lag10, lag11, lag12, lag13, lag14
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

dim(lag_corr_data)
head(lag_corr_data)

# STEP 2 — Compute Pearson Correlation Matrix

corr_matrix <- cor(
  lag_corr_data,
  method = "pearson",
  use = "complete.obs"
)

round(corr_matrix, 3)

# STEP 3 — Extract Correlation Between Rainfall Lags and WQI

wqi_corr <- corr_matrix[, "WQI"]

round(wqi_corr, 3)

# STEP 4 — Construct Correlation Summary Table

wqi_corr_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(wqi_corr),
  Correlation = as.numeric(wqi_corr)
) %>%
  filter(
    Variable != "WQI"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Lag_Day = as.numeric(
      gsub("lag", "", Variable)
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(Lag_Day)

wqi_corr_df
```

```{r}
# STEP 5 — Rank Lag Correlations by Absolute Strength

corr_rank <- wqi_corr_df %>%
  mutate(
    Abs_Correlation = abs(Correlation)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Abs_Correlation))

corr_rank

write.csv(
  corr_rank,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Rainfall_WQI_Correlation_Ranking.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 8 — Lagged Rainfall–WQI Correlation Structure
# =====================================================

# STEP 6 — Visualize Correlation Across Lag Days

ggplot(
  wqi_corr_df,
  aes(
    x = Lag_Day,
    y = Correlation
  )
) +
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 1,
    colour = "steelblue"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    colour = "darkred"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 0:14
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Lagged Rainfall–WQI Correlation Structure",
    x = "Lag Day",
    y = "Pearson Correlation"
  )
```

```{r}
# STEP 7 — Prepare Dataset for LOESS Analysis

loess_data <- final_matched_df %>%
  select(
    WQI,
    lag0, lag1, lag2, lag3, lag4,
    lag5, lag6, lag7, lag8, lag9,
    lag10, lag11, lag12, lag13, lag14
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("lag"),
    names_to = "Lag_Type",
    values_to = "Rainfall"
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

head(loess_data)

# STEP 8 — Order Lag Variables

loess_data$Lag_Type <- factor(
  loess_data$Lag_Type,
  levels = paste0("lag", 0:14)
)

# STEP 9 — LOESS Response Relationship Visualization

ggplot(
  loess_data,
  aes(
    x = Rainfall,
    y = WQI
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    alpha = 0.25,
    size = 1
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    linewidth = 1,
    colour = "blue"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Lag_Type,
    scales = "free_x",
    ncol = 5
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "LOESS Response Between Lagged Rainfall and WQI",
    x = "Rainfall (mm)",
    y = "WQI"
  )
```

# 4.3
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.3 Spatial Heterogeneity Analysis
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Filter Monitoring Stations for Spatial Analysis

final_cluster_df <- final_matched_df %>%
  filter(
    Station_ID1 %in% selected_stations$Station
  )

# STEP 2 — Construct Station-level Water Quality Features

cluster_data <- final_cluster_df %>%
  group_by(Station_ID1) %>%
  summarise(
    pH  = mean(PH,  na.rm = TRUE),
    DO  = mean(DO,  na.rm = TRUE),
    BOD = mean(BOD, na.rm = TRUE),
    COD = mean(COD, na.rm = TRUE),
    AN  = mean(AN,  na.rm = TRUE),
    SS  = mean(SS,  na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  na.omit()

# STEP 3 — Standardize Water Quality Features

cluster_scaled <- cluster_data %>%
  select(-Station_ID1) %>%
  scale()

rownames(cluster_scaled) <- cluster_data$Station_ID1

# STEP 4 — Compute Distance Matrix

dist_mat <- dist(cluster_scaled)

# STEP 5 — Hierarchical Agglomerative Clustering (Ward’s Method)

hc <- hclust(
  dist_mat, method = "ward.D2"
)

# STEP 6 — Visualize Dendrogram

plot(
  hc,
  main = "Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram",
  xlab = "",
  sub = "",
  cex = 0.8
)

# STEP 7 — Silhouette-based Cluster Validation

fviz_nbclust(
  cluster_scaled, FUN = hcut,
  method = "silhouette",
  k.max = 8
)
```

```{r}
# STEP 8 — Assign Cluster Labels

k <- 4

cluster_data$Cluster <- cutree(
  hc,
  k = k
)

# STEP 9 — Merge Spatial Coordinates

station_clustered <- cluster_data %>%
  left_join(
    station_loc %>%
      select(Station, Lat, Lon),
    by = c("Station_ID1" = "Station")
  )

# STEP 10 — Construct Final Cluster Table

cluster_table <- station_clustered %>%
  mutate(
    Station_No = row_number()
  ) %>%
  select(
    Station_No, Station_ID1, Cluster, Lat, Lon
  )

cluster_table

# STEP 11 — Export Cluster Table

write.csv(
  cluster_table,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/1Cluster_Table.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# VISUALIZATION 9 — Spatial Distribution of Water Quality Clusters
# =====================================================

library(leaflet)

df <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/1Cluster_Table.csv",
  encoding = "UTF-8"
)

df$Cluster <- as.factor(df$Cluster)

colors <- c("#1E3A8A", "#F97316", "#10B981", "#DC2626")
pal <- colorFactor(palette = colors, domain = df$Cluster)

leaflet(df) %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~Lon,
    lat = ~Lat,
    color = ~pal(Cluster),
    fillColor = ~pal(Cluster),
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    radius = 7,
    weight = 1,
    popup = ~paste(
      "<b>Station ID:</b>", Station_ID1, "<br>",
      "<b>Cluster:</b>", Cluster
    )
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    pal = pal,
    values = ~Cluster,
    title = "Cluster",
    position = "bottomright"
  )
```

```{r}
# STEP 12 — Cluster-wise Water Quality Profile

cluster_summary_raw <- station_clustered %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n_station = n(),
    pH_mean  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
    DO_mean  = mean(DO,  na.rm = TRUE),
    BOD_mean = mean(BOD, na.rm = TRUE),
    COD_mean = mean(COD, na.rm = TRUE),
    AN_mean  = mean(AN,  na.rm = TRUE),
    SS_mean  = mean(SS,  na.rm = TRUE),
    organic_load = mean(BOD + COD + AN, na.rm = TRUE),
    solid_load = mean(SS, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

cluster_summary_raw

# STEP 13 — Export Cluster Profile

write.csv(
  cluster_summary_raw,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Cluster_Profile_Raw.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# STEP 14 — Merge WQI with Cluster Labels

df_cluster_wqi <- final_matched_df %>%
  left_join(
    cluster_table %>% select(Station_ID1, Cluster),
    by = "Station_ID1"
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

head(df_cluster_wqi)

# STEP 15 — Station-Cluster Mapping Table

station_cluster_table <- cluster_table %>%
  select(Station_ID1, Cluster) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  arrange(Cluster, Station_ID1)

write.csv(
  station_cluster_table,
  "C:/Users/1/Desktop/Station_Cluster_Table.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# STEP 16 — Cluster-wise WQI Statistics

cluster_wqi_stats <- df_cluster_wqi %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_WQI = mean(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median_WQI = median(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_WQI = sd(WQI, na.rm = TRUE),
    N = n()
  )

# STEP 17 — Classify Water Quality (DOE Standard)

cluster_wqi_class <- cluster_wqi_stats %>%
  mutate(
    DOE_Class = case_when(
      Mean_WQI >= 92.7 ~ "Class I",
      Mean_WQI >= 76.5 & Mean_WQI < 92.7 ~ "Class II",
      Mean_WQI >= 51.9 & Mean_WQI < 76.5 ~ "Class III",
      Mean_WQI >= 31.0 & Mean_WQI < 51.9 ~ "Class IV",
      TRUE ~ "Class V"
    )
  )

cluster_wqi_class
```

# 4.4
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.4 Machine Learning Model Comparison for WQI Prediction
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Construct Modeling Dataset

model_df <- final_matched_df %>%
  filter(
    Station_ID1 %in% selected_stations$Station
  ) %>%
  select(
    WQI,
    Station_ID1,
    lag0, lag2, lag3, lag6, lag8, lag10,
    TM60
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

# STEP 2 — Train/Test Split (80/20)

index <- createDataPartition(
  model_df$WQI, p = 0.8, list = FALSE
)

train <- model_df[index, ]
test  <- model_df[-index, ]

dim(train)
dim(test)

# STEP 3 — Feature Standardization

preproc <- preProcess(
  train[, -1],
  method = c("center", "scale")
)

train_scaled <- predict(preproc, train)
test_scaled  <- predict(preproc, test)

# STEP 4 — Cross-validation Strategy

ctrl <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 5
)

set.seed(246896728)
```

```{r}
# STEP 5 — Model Evaluation Metrics

RMSE <- function(obs, pred) {
  sqrt(mean((obs - pred)^2))
}

MAE <- function(obs, pred) {
  mean(abs(obs - pred))
}

NSE <- function(obs, pred) {
  1 - sum((obs - pred)^2) /
    sum((obs - mean(obs))^2)
}

NMSE <- function(obs, pred) {
  mean((obs - pred)^2) / var(obs)
}
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 6 — Random Forest Model

rf_model <- train(
  WQI ~ .,
  data = train_scaled,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneGrid = data.frame(mtry = 3),
  ntree = 200,
  importance = TRUE
)

rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, test_scaled)

rf_result <- data.frame(
  Model = "Random Forest",
  RMSE = RMSE(test_scaled$WQI, rf_pred),
  MAE  = MAE(test_scaled$WQI, rf_pred),
  NSE  = NSE(test_scaled$WQI, rf_pred),
  NMSE = NMSE(test_scaled$WQI, rf_pred)
)

rf_result
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 7 — Support Vector Regression

svr_model <- train(
  WQI ~ .,
  data = train_scaled,
  method = "svmRadial",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    sigma = 0.05,
    C = 10
  )
)

svr_pred <- predict(svr_model, test_scaled)

svr_result <- data.frame(
  Model = "SVR",
  RMSE = RMSE(test_scaled$WQI, svr_pred),
  MAE  = MAE(test_scaled$WQI, svr_pred),
  NSE  = NSE(test_scaled$WQI, svr_pred),
  NMSE = NMSE(test_scaled$WQI, svr_pred)
)

svr_result
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 8 — Artificial Neural Network

ann_model <- train(
  WQI ~ .,
  data = train_scaled,
  method = "nnet",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneLength = 3,
  linout = TRUE,
  trace = FALSE,
  maxit = 200
)

ann_pred <- predict(ann_model, test_scaled)

ann_result <- data.frame(
  Model = "ANN",
  RMSE = RMSE(test_scaled$WQI, ann_pred),
  MAE  = MAE(test_scaled$WQI, ann_pred),
  NSE  = NSE(test_scaled$WQI, ann_pred),
  NMSE = NMSE(test_scaled$WQI, ann_pred)
)

ann_result
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 9 — Model Performance Comparison

results <- rbind(
  rf_result,
  svr_result,
  ann_result
)

print(results)

# STEP 10 — Extract Best Hyperparameters

rf_model$bestTune
svr_model$bestTune
ann_model$bestTune
```

# 4.5
```{r}
# =====================================================
# 4.5 SHAP Explainability Analysis for WQI Prediction
# =====================================================

# STEP 1 — Remove Non-Numeric Identifier Variable

train_scaled <- train_scaled %>%
  select(-Station_ID1)

test_scaled <- test_scaled %>%
  select(-Station_ID1)

# STEP 2 — Refit ANN Model for SHAP Analysis

ann_model <- train(
  WQI ~ .,
  data = train_scaled,
  method = "nnet",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneLength = 3,
  linout = TRUE,
  trace = FALSE,
  maxit = 200
)

# STEP 3 — Define Feature Matrices for SHAP

X_train <- train_scaled %>% select(-WQI)
X_test  <- test_scaled %>% select(-WQI)

# STEP 4 — Construct Background Dataset for Kernel SHAP

bg_X <- X_train %>%
  slice_sample(n = min(200, nrow(X_train)))

# STEP 5 — Compute Kernel SHAP Values

ks <- kernelshap(
  object = ann_model,
  X = X_test,
  bg_X = bg_X
)

shp <- shapviz(ks)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 6 — Global Feature Importance (SHAP)

p_bar <- sv_importance(
  shp,
  kind = "bar"
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Global SHAP Importance for WQI Prediction"
  )

print(p_bar)

# STEP 7 — SHAP Beeswarm Plot

p_beeswarm <- sv_importance(
  shp,
  kind = "beeswarm"
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Beeswarm Plot for WQI Prediction"
  )

print(p_beeswarm)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 8 — Extract SHAP Values

shap_values <- as.data.frame(ks$S)

head(shap_values)

# STEP 9 — Attach Cluster Labels to Test Data

cluster_test <- test %>%
  select(Station_ID1) %>%
  left_join(
    cluster_table %>%
      select(Station_ID1, Cluster),
    by = "Station_ID1"
  )

head(cluster_test)

# STEP 10 — Merge SHAP Values with Cluster Labels

shap_cluster <- bind_cols(
  cluster_test,
  shap_values
)

head(shap_cluster)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 11 — Compute Mean Absolute SHAP by Cluster

cluster_shap_summary <- shap_cluster %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    lag0  = mean(abs(lag0)),
    lag2  = mean(abs(lag2)),
    lag3  = mean(abs(lag3)),
    lag6  = mean(abs(lag6)),
    lag8  = mean(abs(lag8)),
    lag10 = mean(abs(lag10)),
    TM60  = mean(abs(TM60))
  )

cluster_shap_summary
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 12 — Cluster-wise SHAP Importance Plot

library(tidyverse)

cluster_shap_long <- cluster_shap_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Cluster,
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "SHAP"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = factor(
      Variable,
      levels = c("lag0", "lag2", "lag3", "lag6", "lag8", "lag10", "TM60")
    )
  )

ggplot(
  cluster_shap_long,
  aes(
    x = Variable,
    y = SHAP,
    fill = factor(Cluster)
  )
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Variable",
    y = "Mean Absolute SHAP Value",
    fill = "Cluster"
  )
```

```{r}
# =====================================================
# STEP 13 — Identify Dominant Lag per Cluster

cluster_lag_long <- cluster_shap_summary %>%
  select(
    Cluster,
    lag0,
    lag2,
    lag3,
    lag6,
    lag8,
    lag10
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    -Cluster,
    names_to = "Lag",
    values_to = "SHAP"
  )

dominant_lag <- cluster_lag_long %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  slice_max(
    SHAP,
    n = 1,
    with_ties = FALSE
  )

dominant_lag
```
